conflicts.description: Chapter 8 gives Anita and Meera a March or early-April defense expectation, but chapter 15 shows the committee refusing to schedule any defense date.
conflicts.canonical_value: No defense month is set; the committee defers scheduling in chapter 15 and gives no new date.
conflicts.resolution_note: The chapter 8 month is an in-story lie told to family under pressure. Chapter 15 changes the actual continuity state and is more causally binding.

conflicts.description: The Baltimore chapters establish that `ruderal_pitch_v3.pdf` uses unpublished draft student figures and labels; in chapter 17 Mohit publicly calls it an approved aggregate mock-up.
conflicts.canonical_value: `ruderal_pitch_v3.pdf` contains unsubmitted draft student work from June and Leela and was printed for real outside-industry meetings.
conflicts.resolution_note: The document's typo, draft labels, print receipt, and June/Ben corroboration are more deeply embedded than Mohit's sponsor-facing explanation.

conflicts.description: The previous continuity sheet said June temporarily withdrew from the grievance coalition in chapter 14. The manuscript shows her taking Mohit's meeting and off-ramp, needing room, and later publicly backing the evidence in chapter 17; there is no clean on-page formal withdrawal.
conflicts.canonical_value: June accepts Mohit's industry off-ramp and distances herself after chapter 14, but she does not make a clean formal withdrawal and publicly corroborates the evidence in chapter 17.
conflicts.resolution_note: This wording preserves both her chapter 14 self-protective ambiguity and her chapter 17 public choice.

conflicts.description: The previous continuity sheet framed chapter 7 mainly as a data-handling complaint tied to cloned repositories. The manuscript specifies an interim Hades access restriction tied to shared privileged credentials and broader access-governance failure, with no formal misconduct finding.
conflicts.canonical_value: Leela loses `hades-admin` access pending review of shared privileged credentials and repository replication, without a formal misconduct finding.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript's procedural language is more specific and less accusatory than the older sheet wording, so the sheet is updated to match it.
